title: No smartphones, internet or AI: This Karnataka boy from Chikkamagaluru, who learnt about space from encycl - The Economic Times
full_text:
The journey of Awais Ahmed shows how a simple interest in science, even without access to modern technology, can lead to groundbreaking ideas. Years before smartphones, fast internet and artificial intelligence tools became common, books became his source of knowledge and inspiration.
A childhood powered by books and questions
Awais Ahmed grew up in Aldur village in Karnataka's Chikkamagaluru district, where internet access was not part of everyday life during his early years. He discovered the world of space through encyclopaedias that his father brought home.Those books introduced him to galaxies, planets and the mysteries of the universe. With no online videos or instant answers available, his curiosity grew through reading and imagination.
His interest continued during his college years at BITS Pilani, where he studied Mathematics. He joined Team Anant, the institute's student satellite project developed with ISRO, and later became the engineering lead of Hyperloop India, which participated in the SpaceX Hyperloop Pod Competition.
A problem in satellite data led to Pixxel
The idea behind Pixxel began in 2018 when Awais and his BITS Pilani batchmate Kshitij Khandelwal were working on the IBM Watson AI Challenge.Traditional satellites could capture Earth images, but they often missed smaller changes that were invisible to normal observation. Early signs of crop damage, methane emissions, industrial pollution and other environmental issues could go unnoticed.
Instead of searching for existing solutions, the two decided to create one.
In February 2019, they launched Pixxel while still in their early twenties. They used money borrowed from Awais's father and managed their early days with limited resources, spending around Rs 10,000 per month.
From a small startup to a global space company
Pixxel has grown significantly since its early days and has attracted investments from global names including Google, Radical Ventures and Lightspeed.The company has raised around $95 million and has become one of the most funded hyperspectral imaging companies in the world.
In 2025, Pixxel placed all six of its Firefly satellites into orbit. These satellites are designed to collect detailed information about Earth by observing more than 250 spectral bands, providing far more data compared with traditional Earth observation satellites.
The technology can support several real-world uses. Farmers can identify crop stress before visible damage appears, companies can detect methane leaks, authorities can monitor illegal mining and researchers can track environmental changes.
Recognition beyond India
Pixxel's work has received international attention. TIME included the company in its 100 Best Inventions of 2023, while the World Economic Forum recognised it as a Technology Pioneer in 2024.The company also became the first Indian space startup to secure a contract with NASA and later signed a five-year agreement with the US National Reconnaissance Office.
Awais Ahmed has also been recognised through platforms such as Forbes 30 Under 30, MIT Innovators Under 35 and Fortune India's 40 Under 40. His co-founder Kshitij Khandelwal has also featured in the Forbes 30 Under 30 list.
From a village library of ideas to Earth's orbit
For many students, the biggest lesson from Awais Ahmed's story is not only about satellites or technology. It is about where ideas can come from.He did not begin his journey with expensive gadgets or unlimited access to digital tools. His first connection with space came through books and curiosity.
The same curiosity that started with encyclopaedias in a small Karnataka village eventually helped create satellites looking back at Earth from space.

title: Patna schools closed till June 30 for Classes 8 and below due to heatwave; DM issues fresh order - The Times of India
full_text:
Patna schools closed till June 30 for Classes 8 and below due to heatwave; DM issues fresh order
According to the order issued on June 28, the district administration said that the ongoing heatwave and high temperatures continue to pose a serious threat to the health and safety of school-going children. Keeping this in mind, it decided to extend the restrictions on physical academic activities.
The order has been issued under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 2023. It also follows an earlier order issued on June 21, 2026, under Memo No. 8706/Legal, through which similar restrictions had already been imposed because of the intense heat.
Under the latest directive, academic activities for students from pre-school to Class 8 will remain suspended in all government and private schools across Patna district till June 30, 2026.
School authorities have been instructed to reschedule their academic activities in line with the district administration's latest directions. While regular classroom teaching for these classes has been suspended during this period, schools have been asked to ensure compliance with the revised schedule.
Decision taken to ensure children's safety
District Magistrate Kundan Kumar said the decision was taken after the administration found that the prevailing weather conditions continued to endanger the health and lives of young children. As the heatwave persists across the district, suspending academic activities for younger students was considered a necessary precaution.
The district administration has directed all government and private schools to strictly follow the order during the specified period. The decision comes as several parts of Bihar continue to experience extremely high temperatures, prompting authorities to take preventive steps to reduce heat-related risks for schoolchildren.

title: RT’s Irumudi Kattu: Experience Devotional Poonakaalu! - Gulte
full_text:
The musical campaign of Mass Maharaja Ravi Teja’s Irumudi has begun on an extraordinary note with the release of Irumudi Kattu, a devotional anthem that perfectly reflects the film’s spiritual core. Helmed by Shiva Nirvana and bankrolled by Mythri Movie Makers, the film has been generating strong buzz ever since its first glimpse, and the first single has now taken the excitement several notches higher.
GV Prakash Kumar once again proves why he is enjoying one of the finest phases of his career. His composition blends traditional devotional elements with rich orchestration, creating a spiritually charged number that evokes reverence and delivers devotional poonakaalu throughout its runtime.
The lyrics, jointly written by Shiva Nirvana and Kalyan Chakravarthy, beautifully portray the life-changing experience of an Ayyappa devotee. From embracing the sacred Mandala Deeksha to carrying the Irumudi and finally embarking on the holy trek to Sabarimala, every emotion is expressed with remarkable genuineness. Ananthu’s passionate rendition elevates the composition further, making the song spiritually fulfilling.
Ravi Teja undergoes a striking transformation. Shedding his usual mass image, he convincingly steps into the role of a devoted Ayyappa follower, bringing remarkable sincerity and restraint to his performance. Baba Baskar complements this portrayal with graceful choreography inspired by traditional devotional practices. The song also offers glimpses into the protagonist’s family life, introducing Priya Bhavani Shankar as his wife and Baby Nakshatra as his daughter.
As the first single, Irumudi Kattu sets the perfect tone for the album and is sure to echo through Ayyappa Poojas everywhere.

title: RAPO’s Long Cherished Vision Officially Came To Life - Gulte
full_text:
There are projects that entertain, and then there are projects born from years of relentless passion. For Energetic Star Ram Pothineni, #RAPO23 belongs firmly in the latter category. His long-cherished vision officially came to life today with a traditional pooja ceremony of #RAPO23, marking the beginning of a path-breaking film that sees him wear multiple creative hats. Krishna Pothineni is producing the prestigious venture under the Rapo Cinematics banner.
As per tradition, producer Krishna Pothineni handed over the bound script to Ram Pothineni. The muhurtham shot was initiated with P Murali Mohan switching on the camera and PV Ravi Kishore sounding the clapboard. The highlight of the event was Ram Pothineni taking charge of the very first shot, officially embarking on his directorial journey. The celebrations were immediately followed by the commencement of regular shooting.
The film’s story was written by Ram himself. Conceived as a psychological action thriller set in a gripping neo-noir universe, the film aims to introduce to a fresh cinematic experience packed with emotional intensity, mystery, and compelling character journeys.
Ram’s involvement extends far beyond acting. As the film’s writer, director, and leading man, he is personally looking after every aspect of the project, making it one of the most creatively demanding undertakings of his career. It also marks his highest budgeted movie to date.
National Award-winning cinematographer Thirru brings his rich visual expertise, while Peter Hein is designing spectacular action sequences.
With its groundbreaking story, stellar technical support, and a talented cast soon to be unveiled, #RAPO23 promises to stand apart as one of the most distinctive and ambitious films in contemporary Telugu cinema.

title: Peddi’s Smart OTT Move, Others Should Follow! - Gulte
full_text:
We at Gulte have exclusively learned that Peddi is set to premiere on Netflix from July 16 after enjoying an extended theatrical run. If the date is confirmed, it would be a sensible decision by the makers, as maintaining a healthy gap between theatrical and OTT release benefits both exhibitors and the film industry.
In recent years, many films have arrived on streaming platforms within just a few weeks of their theatrical release, reducing the incentive for audiences to visit cinemas. A longer theatrical window allows films to maximize their box office potential while preserving the excitement of the big-screen experience.
Peddi is a good example of why this strategy works. Even though the film’s collections have naturally slowed over time, it continues to attract audiences. On Sunday alone, the film reportedly collected around ₹1.5 crore from the Telugu states, which is a respectable figure at this stage of its theatrical run.
These steady collections show that the film still has an audience willing to watch it in cinemas. Regardless of whether a film receives positive or mixed word of mouth, producers should consider maintaining a reasonable theatrical window before releasing it on OTT.
A balanced release strategy benefits theatres, distributors, and producers while ensuring audiences continue to value the theatrical experience. Peddi could serve as a positive example for future releases.
